memes.label: Amy Belden
memes.description: A woman who runs a boarding house and becomes an important witness in the later stages of the investigation; her testimony connects several key threads of the case.

memes.label: Bullet
memes.description: The projectile that killed Horatio Leavenworth; the central piece of physical evidence examined at the coroner's inquest and tracked throughout the investigation in The Leavenworth Case.

memes.label: Circumstantial Evidence
memes.description: A legal principle in 19th-century criminal law where guilt is inferred from surrounding facts rather than direct eyewitness testimony; a central theme in The Leavenworth Case.

memes.label: Clue
memes.description: A piece of evidence or information that guides a criminal investigation toward its resolution; the detective's essential working material in The Leavenworth Case.

memes.label: Confession
memes.description: A voluntary admission of guilt by the perpetrator of a crime; the dramatic climax of the investigation in The Leavenworth Case.

memes.label: Coroner's Inquest
memes.description: A formal judicial inquiry conducted by a coroner to determine the cause and circumstances of a death; the critical first investigative proceeding that establishes the legal and evidentiary foundation of The Leavenworth Case.

memes.label: Crime
memes.description: The central unlawful act around which The Leavenworth Case revolves — the murder of Horatio Leavenworth — and the legal and moral framework through which the novel examines guilt, innocence, and justice.

memes.label: Detection
memes.description: The systematic process of discovering the truth of a crime through the collection and analysis of evidence; the professional methodology of Ebenezer Gryce in The Leavenworth Case.

memes.label: Discovery of the Altered Will
memes.description: The discovery that Horatio Leavenworth had altered his will shortly before his death, shifting the inheritance from Mary to Eleanore — a revelation that transforms the investigation's understanding of motive.

memes.label: Ebenezer Gryce
memes.description: The detective assigned to investigate the murder of Horatio Leavenworth; a methodical and shrewd professional who drives the investigation in The Leavenworth Case and one of the earliest detective characters in American fiction.

memes.label: Eleanore Leavenworth
memes.description: The reserved and enigmatic niece of Horatio Leavenworth, whose unexplained silence following the murder and a secret marriage make her a primary subject of investigation in The Leavenworth Case.

memes.label: "Endnotes"
memes.description: "Endnotes for The Leavenworth Case"

memes.label: Everett Raymond
memes.description: A junior partner at the law firm Veeley, Carr & Raymond and the first-person narrator of The Leavenworth Case; his observations and personal involvement with the Leavenworth sisters drive the novel's dual narrative of detection and romance.

memes.label: Evidence
memes.description: The body of facts, testimony, and physical objects presented at the coroner's inquest and gathered by Detective Gryce to determine the perpetrator of the Leavenworth murder; the central currency of the novel's investigative logic.

memes.label: Examination
memes.description: The formal questioning of a witness under oath in a legal proceeding; a critical procedure in the coroner's inquest and investigation in The Leavenworth Case.

memes.label: Hamlet
memes.description: A tragedy by William Shakespeare referenced in chapter epigraphs in The Leavenworth Case; its themes of investigation, feigned madness, and the moral burden of truth-seeking parallel the novel's detective narrative.

memes.label: Handkerchief Evidence
memes.description: A handkerchief stained with pistol smoke and grease, introduced as physical evidence at the coroner's inquest; a pivotal piece of circumstantial evidence in The Leavenworth Case.

memes.label: Hannah Chester
memes.description: A servant in the Leavenworth household whose observations and testimony provide crucial domestic details about the night of the murder.

memes.label: Henry Clavering
memes.description: An Englishman whose secret marriage to Eleanore Leavenworth and mysterious presence near the Leavenworth mansion on the night of the murder make him a key figure in the investigation in The Leavenworth Case.

memes.label: Hoffman House
memes.description: A prominent New York hotel whose register and staff testimony become crucial evidence in tracing the movements of Henry Clavering during the Leavenworth murder investigation.

memes.label: Horatio Leavenworth
memes.description: A wealthy New York gentleman whose murder sets the entire investigation in motion; his will, his relationships, and his death are the central facts of The Leavenworth Case.

memes.label: Inheritance and the Will
memes.description: The legal transmission of property upon death according to the terms of a will; the financial framework that establishes the primary motive for murder in The Leavenworth Case.

memes.label: Interview
memes.description: The formal and informal investigative conversations through which Detective Gryce, Everett Raymond, and the coroner gather information, test hypotheses, and apply pressure to suspects in The Leavenworth Case.

memes.label: Jury
memes.description: A group of citizens sworn to render a verdict based on evidence presented in a legal proceeding; the fact-finding body at the coroner's inquest whose verdict of murder activates the criminal investigation in The Leavenworth Case.

memes.label: Justice
memes.description: The principle of moral rightness and legal fairness that governs the investigation and its resolution; a central thematic concern in The Leavenworth Case as characters weigh legal guilt against moral culpability.

memes.label: Leavenworth Mansion
memes.description: The Fifth Avenue residence of Horatio Leavenworth; the primary setting where the murder occurs and the investigation begins.

memes.label: Letter
memes.description: Written correspondence in The Leavenworth Case that serves as a crucial form of documentary evidence, including the mysterious letter from Henry Clavering that had a "marked effect" on the Leavenworth household shortly before the murder.

memes.label: Macbeth
memes.description: A tragedy by William Shakespeare referenced through chapter epigraphs in The Leavenworth Case; its treatment of murder, guilt, and the impossibility of concealing crime provides a literary counterpoint to the novel's central concerns.

memes.label: Mary Leavenworth
memes.description: The beautiful and composed niece of Horatio Leavenworth, initially declared his sole heiress; the contradictions in her testimony and the altered will make her the central suspect in The Leavenworth Case.

memes.label: Mary's Confession
memes.description: The climactic confession by Mary Leavenworth that resolves the murder investigation; a dramatic revelation of guilt, motive, and circumstance that transforms the understanding of the entire case in The Leavenworth Case.

memes.label: The Merchant of Venice
memes.description: A play by William Shakespeare referenced in The Leavenworth Case; its themes of justice, mercy, and the letter of the law provide a legal-philosophical counterpoint to the novel's treatment of crime and punishment.

memes.label: Motive
memes.description: In criminal investigation, the reason or incentive that drives a person to commit a crime; a critical element of proof in The Leavenworth Case.

memes.label: Mr. Clavering Sr.
memes.description: The father of Henry Clavering, whose social standing and connection to the family provides context for the secret marriage plot in The Leavenworth Case.

memes.label: Mr. Veeley
memes.description: The senior partner at Veeley, Carr & Raymond and the legal representative of the Leavenworth family; his professional relationship with the deceased connects the law firm to the case.

memes.label: Mrs. Belden
memes.description: A landlady whose boarding house becomes an important location in the investigation; her detailed narrative testimony provides crucial evidence connecting key figures in the case.

memes.label: Murder Weapon
memes.description: The instrument used to commit a homicide; in The Leavenworth Case, a small Smith & Wesson pistol that becomes a critical piece of physical evidence in the investigation.

memes.label: Mystery
memes.description: The term used throughout The Leavenworth Case to refer to the unsolved murder and its surrounding circumstances — a narrative and investigative framework that structures the entire novel as a puzzle to be solved through the systematic application of detective method.

memes.label: New York City
memes.description: The primary setting of The Leavenworth Case; the novel unfolds across Manhattan's mansions, hotels, law offices, and churches, depicting the interconnected social geography of 1870s New York.

memes.label: New York Police Department
memes.description: The municipal police force employing Detective Ebenezer Gryce; the institutional framework that provides resources, authority, and infrastructure for the murder investigation in The Leavenworth Case.

memes.label: Poison
memes.description: A toxic substance capable of causing death; in The Leavenworth Case, the question of poisoning emerges as a significant investigative lead connected to a key witness's death.

memes.label: Portland Place
memes.description: A location connected to Eleanore Leavenworth; significant in the later stages of the investigation.

memes.label: Proof
memes.description: The establishment of a fact by evidence that excludes reasonable doubt; the ultimate standard to which all investigation aspires in The Leavenworth Case.

memes.label: Q
memes.description: A subordinate detective working under Ebenezer Gryce, described as a "living interrogation point"; his role in the investigation involves gathering intelligence and tracking suspects.

memes.label: R— (Town)
memes.description: A location outside New York City connected to the later stages of the investigation and the background of certain characters.

memes.label: Romeo and Juliet
memes.description: A tragedy by William Shakespeare referenced in The Leavenworth Case; its theme of secret love thwarted by family opposition resonates with the novel's secret marriage plot.

memes.label: Secret Marriage
memes.description: A marriage performed in private, without public notice or family knowledge; a central plot element in The Leavenworth Case that provides the motive for the crime.

memes.label: Suspicion
memes.description: A state of belief or conjecture that a particular person may be guilty of a crime, based on partial or inconclusive evidence; a pervasive psychological force in The Leavenworth Case.

memes.label: Telegram
memes.description: An electrical telegraph message used for rapid long-distance communication; telegrams in The Leavenworth Case serve as crucial evidence connecting characters across distances and revealing hidden relationships.

memes.label: Testimony
memes.description: Sworn statements given by witnesses under oath at a legal proceeding; the primary form of evidence presented at the coroner's inquest in The Leavenworth Case.

memes.label: The Butler
memes.description: The butler of the Leavenworth household; his testimony about the pistol and his recognition of the murder weapon provide a key moment in the coroner's inquest.

memes.label: The Coroner's Inquest
memes.description: The formal judicial inquiry held at the Leavenworth mansion on the day after the murder discovery; the critical event that establishes the legal and evidentiary foundation for the entire criminal investigation in The Leavenworth Case.

memes.label: The Coroner's Office
memes.description: The municipal office responsible for investigating suspicious deaths in New York City; the institutional authority behind the coroner's inquest into Horatio Leavenworth's death that authorizes the criminal investigation.

memes.label: The Murder of Horatio Leavenworth
memes.description: The central event of the novel: the fatal shooting of wealthy New York gentleman Horatio Leavenworth in his own library on the night of March 4, 187–.

memes.label: The Secret Marriage
memes.description: The clandestine marriage between Eleanore Leavenworth and Henry Clavering, revealed during the murder investigation and central to understanding the motive for the crime.

memes.label: The Coroner
memes.description: The official who presides over the coroner's inquest into the death of Horatio Leavenworth; his professional competence establishes the legal foundation for the criminal investigation.

memes.label: Trinity Church
memes.description: A New York church where the secret marriage between Eleanore Leavenworth and Henry Clavering may have been solemnized.

memes.label: Troilus and Cressida
memes.description: A play by William Shakespeare referenced in The Leavenworth Case; its themes of betrayal and disillusionment provide epigraphic commentary on the novel's action.

memes.label: Trueman Harwell
memes.description: The private secretary of Horatio Leavenworth; his testimony at the coroner's inquest provides essential information about the household on the night of the murder.

memes.label: Veeley, Carr & Raymond
memes.description: The New York law firm that represents the Leavenworth family, drafts Horatio Leavenworth's will, and employs the narrator Everett Raymond.

memes.label: Verdict
memes.description: The formal decision rendered by a jury at the conclusion of a legal proceeding; the determination of murder at the coroner's inquest in The Leavenworth Case.

memes.label: Witness Testimony
memes.description: A formal statement made under oath by a witness in a legal proceeding; the primary form of evidence presented at the coroner's inquest and throughout the investigation in The Leavenworth Case.
